title: DC-area forecast: Isolated storm tonight but threat of severe weather Tuesday
subtitle: HAPPENING NOW: It's a nice evening with modest humidity and temperatures slowly falling through the 80s.
contentHtml:
Always a human at the helm: Updated around-the-clock by Capital Weather meteorologists.

Happening now: It's a nice evening with modest humidity and temperatures slowly falling through the 80s.

What’s next? Humidity returns tonight and ramps up Tuesday, when strong to severe storms may develop late as a cold front approaches. A few lingering showers or storms are possible Wednesday before a stretch of pleasant weather Thursday into the weekend.

Forecast in detail

Tonight: It gradually turns muggier and clouds also increase. Spotty showers or thunderstorms are possible late tonight into early Tuesday morning as a warm front lifts north through the area. Lows range from 65 to 70. Confidence: Medium-High

Hourly Forecast | Pollen, Air & UV

Tomorrow (Tuesday): A morning shower or thunderstorm is possible before skies brighten. It turns very humid (dew points climbing into the mid-70s), with highs near 90. Showers and thunderstorms are possible late afternoon into evening, especially between 3 and 9 p.m., and some could become severe. Confidence: Medium

Tomorrow night: Showers and storms, some intense, may linger into the overnight hours. Otherwise, skies are mostly cloudy, with lows near 70. Confidence: Medium

📬 Get our forecast in your inbox

Our morning forecast email is back! It's included with every level of support — from Free to Founder — delivered 7 days a week. Sign up at: capitalweather.com/support-us

A look ahead

Moderately high humidity lingers Wednesday but should gradually decrease during the second half of the day. There's a chance of some showers and storms popping up, mainly east of Interstate 95. Highs range from the mid- to upper 80s. Clearing and more comfortable at night, with lows in the 60s. Confidence: Medium

Quiet and pleasantly comfortable weather Thursday and Friday. Humidity declines and there's plenty of sun. Highs both days should range from the low to mid-80s. Nighttime lows dip into the 60s with even some 50s possible in our cooler suburbs. Confidence: Medium-High

Pretty good weather should continue into the weekend, although humidity ticks up a bit and there's a slight chance of late-day showers and storms. Highs stay in the mid- to upper 80s, with lows in the 60s to around 70. Confidence: Medium

DC weather history for July 20

Extreme heat peaked in 1930 with a record-tying 106 degrees, part of a brutal multi-day stretch of triple-digit temperatures during a severe drought.

Today’s daily digit — 8/10: Worry-free weather today, a welcome change after days of smoke, heat and storms. | 🤚 Your call?
The digit is a somewhat subjective rating of the day’s weather, on a 0-to-10 scale.
